title: SEC2: JSON string roundtrip across WASM boundary for events
description:
File: wasm/src/client.rs lines 241-242.

wire_notify_callback builds a serde_json::Value event, serializes it to a String (serde_json::to_string), then converts that to JsValue::from_str to cross the WASM boundary. The JS caller must then JSON.parse() the string to use it.

Cost: one serde_json serialization allocation + one JS string allocation + one JS JSON.parse() call on every relay notification. Three extra operations per event that could be eliminated.

Fix: Use serde-wasm-bindgen (serde_wasm_bindgen::to_value(&event)) or js_sys::Object construction to pass the event as a native JS object directly. This avoids the String intermediate entirely. Dependency: add serde-wasm-bindgen to wasm/Cargo.toml (it is already common in the wasm-bindgen ecosystem and adds negligible binary size for what it replaces).

Classify: P2 (measurable latency + allocation on every notification event crossing the WASM/JS boundary)
